title: Habitable Zone
shortDescription: A vast warehouse-like level of concrete pillars and non-Euclidean geometry, home to survivor settlements offering food, shelter and guidance.
atmosphere: Industrial and liminal; flickering lights bring dangerous blackouts. Habitable despite roaming entities and deceptive distances.
tips: Navigate by fixed landmarks. Cross corridors during flickering events. Loot crates for Almond Water. Seek established bases.

title: Abandoned Utility Halls
shortDescription: Vast maintenance tunnels of machinery, wiring and mysterious doors, in a Euclidean layout accessible from Level 1 and the Frontrooms.
atmosphere: Sparse fluorescent light, heavy machines choking narrow concrete corridors; abandoned, industrial, dark and silent after a blackout.
tips: Shelter near Office Space EL3A. Never open void rooms behind locked doors. Beware power failures on series-wired lighting.

title: Electrical Station
shortDescription: An abandoned industrial complex, resource-rich but walled by indestructible barriers and hunted by unusually intelligent, coordinated entities.
atmosphere: Dark brick halls of archaic electrical gear, humming machines and black liquid in the pipes; a pervasive sense of being hunted.
tips: Never go alone. Reach Base Gamma. Don't stare at barred areas. Carry a flashlight and a light-bomb against entity brigades.

title: Abandoned Office
shortDescription: An empty office building with minimal furniture: a major hub where wanderers gather and stock up on supplies, especially Almond Water.
atmosphere: Mostly desolate corridors, some windows blacked out; quiet and commercial with moderate traffic from other travelers.
tips: Stock Almond Water before going deeper. Avoid clear windows — they are traps. A good place to meet others and resupply.

title: Terror Hotel
shortDescription: An infinite 1930s hotel of pristine mahogany halls and mysterious rooms, populated by deathmoths and unsettling watching presences.
atmosphere: Eerie elegance: vintage décor, ambient jazz, self-cleaning surfaces, strange whispers and a constant sense of being watched.
tips: Stay with communities like the Homely Hotel. Avoid elevators past floor 12 and dark deathmoth rooms. Carry good meat for Samantha.

title: Lights Out
shortDescription: A completely dark level of seamless concrete hallways where light sources fail and sound is absent, causing severe psychological distress.
atmosphere: Total darkness, absolute silence and deep isolation breed paranoia; auditory hallucinations come with prolonged exposure.
tips: Navigate by touch. Listen for faint waves to find the Level 7 exit. Never flip a discovered light switch.

title: Thalassophobia
shortDescription: An impossibly vast ocean under dim light, split by depth into zones, holding two apex predators and countless humanoid skeletal remains.
atmosphere: Claustrophobic despite open water; perpetual twilight, crushing depths and stray bioluminescence — desolate and isolating.
tips: Use the entrance room as base camp. Exploit the Thing's light sensitivity and Tiny's poor hearing. Keep excursions short.

title: Cave Systems
shortDescription: A vast underground cave network with non-Euclidean geometry, shifting gravity, flooded passages and many hostile entities.
atmosphere: Oppressively dark caverns, cold almond water, eerie silence broken by creature sounds and flickering emergency lights.
tips: Follow the 9th Road markers religiously. Stay warm, dry and awake against hypothermia. Avoid glyph-marked spider colonies.

title: The Suburbs
shortDescription: An infinite suburb frozen at midnight, with furnished houses and deadly foggy streets prowled by hazardous entities.
atmosphere: Dark, eerie neighborhood: midnight fog, flickering streetlights, abandoned homes and unsettling psychological effects.
tips: Follow arrow signs toward Level 11. Avoid the entity-spawning fog. Loot houses but beware sudden teleportation.

title: Bumper Crop
shortDescription: Endless wheat and barley fields under an overcast sky, dotted with empty structures and lakes, with worms burrowing beneath the soil.
atmosphere: Dreary, unchanging pastoral expanse under permanent overcast, calm but for underlying mysteries beneath the fields.
tips: Follow the roads toward Level 11. Gather supplies from empty structures. Dig with caution — the worms surface fast.
